title: Missing negative test for century-club achievement (low TM should not unlock)
description: The achievements test suite has a positive test for century-club (bench TM=200) but no negative test confirming it does NOT trigger when all set weights are below 100 kg. The default benchTM=100 users (TM=90) could serve as a negative baseline.

title: Shareable Workout Card
description: Generate a screenshot-ready card after completing a workout showing: exercises, top sets, total volume, PRs hit, workout duration. Styled with SetForge branding. Share via native share API or download as image. Great for Instagram stories.

title: Nudge / Poke Friend
description: Send a 'get to the gym' nudge to a friend who hasn't trained in X days. One-tap from the friends list. Recipient sees it as a feed event or in-app notification. Rate-limited to 1 nudge per friend per day to prevent spam.

title: User Profile Page
description: Public-ish profile page showing: display name, member since date, current plan, lift stats summary (current TMs or e1RMs), workout streak, badges earned, total workouts completed. Viewable by friends. Foundation for social identity in the app.

title: Progression Race
description: Compare TM progression charts side-by-side with a friend over a selectable time range (30d/90d/all). Pick an exercise and see who's progressing faster. Visual overlay chart on the existing progress page or a dedicated comparison view.

title: Invite Link for Friend Requests
description: Generate a unique invite link (e.g. setforge.app/invite/abc123) that auto-sends a friend request when the invitee registers. Easier than sharing email addresses. Link expires after 7 days or first use. Shareable via native share API.

title: PR Celebration Post
description: When a user hits a personal record (requires PR Detection #008), auto-generate a celebratory feed event with confetti animation. Shows the lift, weight, reps, and how it compares to previous best. Friends see it prominently in their feed.

title: Gym Crew / Groups
description: Create a named group of friends (a 'crew') that shares its own leaderboard and activity feed. Good for real-life gym groups or online communities running the same plan. Crew has a name, members, shared leaderboard, and crew feed.

title: Accountability Partner
description: Pair with one friend as an accountability partner. Both get notified when the other skips a scheduled workout day. Opt-in mutual agreement. Shows partner's schedule and completion status on your dashboard. Designed for casual users who need external motivation.

title: Weekly Activity Digest
description: Weekly summary of friends' activity delivered as a feed event or dedicated view every Sunday. Shows: who trained most, biggest TM jumps, longest active streaks, total group volume. Gives casual users a reason to check in even if they don't open the app daily.

title: Rage Quit Button
description: Bad day? Slam a big red button that discards the workout with a dramatic animation and a random encouraging quote. Uses existing soft-delete (discarded status) under the hood.

title: Workout Wrapped
description: Monthly/yearly Spotify Wrapped-style summary. Total tonnage lifted, favorite day of week, longest streak, biggest PR, fun comparisons ('You lifted the equivalent of 3 Toyota Corollas this month'). Shareable summary cards.

title: Plate Math Speed Challenge
description: Mini-game: given a target weight, tap the plates to load the bar as fast as possible. Leaderboard against yourself. Trains useful gym math skills. Frontend-only game mode.

title: Form Check Reminder
description: Every N workouts at a given weight, nudge: 'You have been at 100kg bench for 6 sessions. Film a form check?' Links to camera. No AI analysis, just the reminder habit to encourage good form.

title: Quick Rep Edit
description: Improve rep entry UX with bigger +/- buttons or a number spinner directly on set rows. Current entry feels like too many taps, especially with sweaty hands between sets.

title: Set Completion Haptic
description: Vibration or sound feedback when a set is logged. Confirms the tap registered, especially useful with sweaty/chalky hands or dim screens.

title: Workout Progress Indicator
description: Progress bar and set counter at top of workout page showing 'X / 17 sets completed'. Bar fills as sets complete, color transitions gray to blue to green. Frontend-only, derived from existing set data.

title: Recent Workout Peek
description: Before starting a workout, show a summary of last session for that day: key weights and reps. Helps users mentally prepare and know what to expect.

title: Set Weight Override
description: Override auto-calculated weight for individual sets during a workout. For when plates don't work out cleanly at your gym, or you want to be conservative on a bad day. Avoids having to change the TM for one-off adjustments.

title: Workout Duration
description: Track and display how long each workout took. Show duration badge on history calendar entries. Helps users plan gym visits around their schedule.

title: Dark Mode
description: System-aware dark mode following device preference, with manual toggle in Settings (System/Light/Dark). Redefine existing CSS custom properties under data-theme attribute. Store preference in localStorage. Instant switch, no reload.

title: Workout Notes
description: Optional free-text notes on workouts. 'Add note' link on workout page opens textarea (max 500 chars), auto-saves on blur. Notes shown in history detail view. Requires new DB column and API update.

title: Failed Set Tracking
description: Explicitly mark a set as failed rather than just logging low reps. Track failure patterns across sessions. Surface warnings when consecutive failures on the same weight suggest a TM reset is needed.

title: Exercise Substitution
description: Swap a plan exercise for an alternative per-session or permanently. Casual users need it when equipment is taken or they have minor soreness. Power users want same TM reference and set scheme preserved, with approved alternatives per exercise.

title: PR Detection
description: Detect personal records in real-time during workouts. PR = more weight at same or higher rep count than ever before for that exercise. Show animated celebration banner on AMRAP completion. New backend endpoint for per-exercise records by rep count.

title: Effort / RPE Logging
description: Log perceived effort after workouts or per-set. Simple mode: post-workout Easy/Good/Hard rating. Advanced mode: per-set RPE 1-10 scale. Track trends over time to detect under-recovery or peaking.

title: Missed Workout Reschedule
description: When a user skips a planned workout day, prompt them: skip it and move on, or do it today? Removes guesswork about what to do after missing a session.

title: Bodyweight Tracking
description: Log bodyweight (daily or per-session) with a trend line chart. Casual users want weekly weigh-ins to see if training is working. Power users want daily logging, strength-to-bodyweight ratios, and correlation with TM progression.

title: Volume Load History
description: Track total volume load (sets x reps x weight) per exercise per session and chart it over time. Volume load is the best proxy for training stress and hypertrophy stimulus. Surfaces plateaus that raw TM numbers miss.

title: Deload Detection
description: Track AMRAP trends and suggest deload when user hits 0-1 reps on progression AMRAP for 3 consecutive workouts. Suggest 10% TM reduction with one-tap apply. New backend stall-check endpoint, no new DB tables needed.

title: Lift Personality Quiz
description: After 4+ weeks of data, generate a lifter personality based on patterns: 'The Grinder' (always hits exact reps), 'The Sandbagging Overachiever' (smashes AMRAPs), 'The Ghost' (disappears then PRs). Shareable personality card.

title: Gym Bro Trash Talk
description: AI-generated motivational/trash-talk notifications before workout days. 'Your bench has been stuck at 80kg for 3 weeks. Are you even trying?' Adjustable sass level from encouraging to savage.

title: Gym Soundtrack Mode
description: Detect tempo of music playing (via mic permission) and pulse the UI to the beat. Rest timer syncs to song endings. Pure vibes, experimental feature.

title: Validate URL params and query strings with Zod
description: Body validation is solid but params/query have gaps: (1) PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/exercises/:id have no isNaN check causing 500s, (2) All :id params allow 0 or negative values, (3) GET /api/workouts/history limit has no upper bound, (4) GET /api/workouts/calendar year has no range bounds, (5) PATCH /api/workouts/:id/sets/:setId never validates :id param. Fix: extend validate middleware or add Zod schemas for params/query on all routes.

title: Head-to-Head Live Workout
description: Real-time workout comparison when two friends train the same plan day simultaneously. See each other's set completion progress live. Requires WebSocket or polling. Shows 'friend is on set 5/17' alongside your own progress.

title: Strength Standards Comparison
description: Compare your lifts against population-level strength standards (beginner/intermediate/advanced/elite) based on bodyweight and sex. Uses well-known strength standard tables. Requires bodyweight input. Shows where you stand for each lift.

title: QR Code Add Friend
description: Generate a personal QR code in the app that encodes your friend request link. Show it at the gym for someone to scan and instantly send a friend request. Uses invite link system (#063). No typing needed - just scan and connect.

title: Add backend test: user cannot react to their own feed event via direct API call
description: The GET /feed endpoint only shows friends' events, so a user cannot see their own events in the UI. However the POST /feed/:eventId/react endpoint only checks friendIds, not event.userId \!== callerId. A user who knows their own feedEventId can call the react endpoint on it. While the feed won't show their own event, it's an untested edge case. Add a backend test that verifies self-reaction returns 404.

title: Add date range filter to streak query to avoid fetching all workout history
description: getStreaksForUsers in social.ts fetches every completed workout for the given user IDs with no date bound. A user with years of history causes a large scan on every GET /api/social/friends and GET /api/social/feed call. The streak only needs the most recent contiguous block; a WHERE completedAt >= NOW() - INTERVAL '365 days' (or similar) would cap the scan with no correctness impact.

title: Fix push notifications: VAPID key format, subscribe schema mismatch, and button touch targets
description:
Push subscribe is broken in real browsers due to multiple issues. Fix all together:

1. VAPID public key format: GET /api/notifications/public-key returns SPKI DER base64url (91 bytes) but PushManager.subscribe() requires raw EC point (65 bytes, 04||x||y). Real browsers throw InvalidAccessError. Fix: return raw EC point from the endpoint, or convert in frontend.

2. Subscribe response schema mismatch: pushSubscribeResponseSchema expects { id, userId, endpoint, createdAt } but backend POST /subscribe returns { ok: true }. Causes ZodError on successful 201, showing 'Failed to enable notifications'. Fix: align schema to match actual API response.

3. Button touch targets: PushNotificationSettings.module.css .actionBtn and .actionBtnSecondary use min-height 2.75rem instead of project-standard 3rem. Update to 3rem.

title: Improve logging coverage across backend services
description: Fill gaps identified in logging analysis where entire subsystems are silent and client-facing errors go unrecorded. Specific improvements: 1) Add warn-level logging for validation failures (Zod errors) and route-level caught errors (400s, 409s). 2) Add debug-level logging in achievement, notification, and feed services. 3) Add Prisma query event logging for slow queries. 4) Log weight calculation steps at debug level.

title: Audit frontend catch blocks for incorrect error message extraction
description: The register page used err.error.message instead of err.message to extract ApiError messages, causing generic fallback messages. Other catch blocks in settings.tsx, FriendsTab.tsx, and workout.$dayNumber.tsx use the same 'error' in err pattern but operate on raw response bodies (not ApiError). These should be audited to confirm they work correctly now that ApiError is used consistently via the api/client.ts layer.

title: CustomWorkoutModal does not validate initialDate on open — future date passed by caller would bypass error state
description: validateDate is only called from the onChange handler, not on modal open. If initialDate is ever a future date the modal opens with no error and Save enabled. Fix: call validateDate(initialDate) inside the open useEffect.

title: Add feed latestComments edge-case tests: exactly 1 comment and zero-event feed
description: The social.test.ts latestComments suite covers 0-comment and 3-comment (truncated to 2) cases but not: (a) exactly 1 comment on an event, (b) the eventIds-empty early-exit branch when the feed returns zero events. Both are handled correctly by the implementation but lack explicit test coverage.

title: docs: DELETE /social/feed/:eventId/comments/:commentId returns 200 with body but docs say 204
description: social.ts line 769 returns res.json({ id: commentId }) with default 200, but api-endpoints.md line 52 documents the response as 204. Fix the doc to say 200 with body { id: number }.

title: routeHelpers.ts: isP2002UsernameViolation uses weaker typeof check vs isPrismaError instanceof Error guard — align both helpers to the same standard
description: isP2002UsernameViolation checks typeof err === 'object' while isPrismaError checks instanceof Error. Both live in routeHelpers.ts. The inconsistency was surfaced by ticket 119 (the two helpers are now used side-by-side in auth.ts). Update isP2002UsernameViolation to use instanceof Error so both helpers apply the same type narrowing.

title: Update react-query-cache.md: progress key returns e1RM not TM history
description: Ticket 127 changed the progress endpoint from TM history to e1RM but react-query-cache.md still references TMs. Update key description and invalidation rule comments.

title: Repoint CI/CD from Railway to Coolify deploy
description: deploy.yml and deploy.sh trigger a Railway redeploy via Railway GraphQL API. Production target is Coolify, which deploys via webhook or registry/git polling. Repoint the pipeline at Coolify (deploy webhook + token) or configure Coolify to pull the ghcr.io image.

title: Document required production env vars for Coolify
description: Backend requires JWT_SECRET, DATABASE_URL, VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY, VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY (optional LOG_LEVEL/PORT) via requireEnv. No .env.example or doc lists them, so a Coolify deployer cannot know what secrets to inject. Add a documented required-vars list.

title: Change account email
description: PATCH /users/me accepts only username; the email login identifier can never be changed. Add email change with re-verification once email infra exists.

title: Exercise library detail (instructions, muscles, video)
description: Exercise model has only name/slug/category/muscleGroup; no description, instructions, cues, equipment, or video, and no exercise-detail route. New users cannot learn unfamiliar lifts. Add exercise reference content + detail view.

title: Unit preference (kg/lbs)
description: App is hardcoded to kg. A large share of the lifting market (US) trains in pounds. Add a user unit preference with display conversion (storage stays kg, round per unit).

title: Scheduled workout-day reminders (push)
description: Push infra fires only for social events. user_plan_schedules already stores which weekday each training day falls on, but there is no time-to-train reminder, the most retention-driving notification for a workout app. Add scheduled-day reminders.

title: Leave plan / no-active-plan state
description: You can switch plans by subscribing to a different one but cannot stop training and return to a no-plan state. Users taking a break have no clean option. Add unsubscribe / no-active-plan handling.

title: User-created custom plans
description: Plans are admin/system-defined only; regular users can only pick public plans. Serious users want to build/tweak their own programs. Add user plan creation/editing.

title: Cap and validate history pagination limit
description: workouts.ts history: limit = parseInt(query.limit) || 10 passed straight to Prisma take with no max cap and no negative validation; limit=9999999 loads all workouts+sets (DoS), page=-1 yields negative skip. Cap at ~50 and validate page/limit.

title: validateQuery middleware for query params
description: middleware/validate.ts only safeParses req.body, leaving query params unvalidated app-wide (leaderboard mode, history page/limit). Add a validateQuery variant and apply to query-driven endpoints.

title: Add DB indexes on hot query columns (WorkoutSet.exerciseId)
description: WorkoutSet.exerciseId is unindexed but e1RM leaderboard and progress filter workout_sets by exerciseId IN (...). These are the heaviest read paths and will table-scan as data grows. Add targeted indexes.

title: Tune React Query gcTime and refetchOnWindowFocus
description: router.tsx sets only staleTime 60s + retry 1. refetchOnWindowFocus defaults true, so every tab refocus refetches feed/calendar/TMs, wasteful network/battery on the mobile PWA. Set refetchOnWindowFocus and gcTime appropriately.

title: Cache headers for hashed static assets
description: app.ts express.static serves the SPA with no maxAge; Vite emits content-hashed filenames safe to cache forever but they revalidate every load. Set maxAge 1y immutable for assets and keep index.html no-cache.

title: axe-core accessibility tests in Playwright
description: 26 E2E specs, none use axe-core/playwright, so a11y regressions ship undetected despite the project's touch-target/semantic-HTML conventions. Add axe checks to key flows.

title: Feed cursor pagination (load more)
description: social.ts feed is take 20 with no cursor/offset and no load-more, so once a user has >20 events older history is unreachable. Add cursor-based pagination.

title: Password reset / forgot password flow
description: No reset-token flow and no email-sending infrastructure. A user who forgets their password is permanently locked out. Requires email infra (also unblocks email verification). Hard launch blocker.

title: Email verification
description: Registration accepts any email with no confirmation. Unverified emails undermine password reset and notifications and allow fake accounts. Add a verification step (depends on email infra).

title: Push Notifications settings section hangs on 'Loading...' with no timeout/fallback when service worker never becomes ready
description: On the Settings page the Push Notifications section stays on 'Loading...' indefinitely when navigator.serviceWorker.ready never resolves (e.g. in dev where the SW is registered PROD-only, or if SW registration fails in prod). There is no timeout or error fallback, so the section is permanently stuck. Add a timeout/error state. Related to ticket 093 (push notifications).

title: Latent: workout createRef never resets, so same-instance day-to-day navigation could skip workout creation
description: In workout.$dayNumber.tsx the createRef guard is never reset, making the dayNumber dep on the create effect effectively dead: once a create has fired for the component instance, navigating to a different day's 'none' state early-returns without creating. Currently masked by the loader running first and the server EXISTING_WORKOUT conflict path. Only matters if free day-to-day navigation without remount becomes a real flow. Low priority. Surfaced during ticket 164 review.

title: Offline set-log queue: flush pending debounce timer before completing a workout
description: A set confirmed within ~300ms of tapping Complete may have a debounce timer that hasn't fired, so its log is neither delivered nor queued when doCompleteWorkout flushes. Flush/await pending debounce timers before completion so progression is computed from fully-delivered data. Surfaced in ticket 136 review (I3).

title: Offline set-log queue: also flush on visibilitychange (iOS Safari online event is unreliable)
description: iOS Safari fires the 'online' event on interface changes, not real reachability, so flush-on-reconnect can lag. Also flush the set-log queue on document visibilitychange->visible. Minor; from ticket 136 review (M4).

title: Handle post-deploy chunk-load 404 for long-lived tabs (reload on dynamic-import failure)
description: With route code-splitting (156) + the SW skipWaiting/clientsClaim precache purge (135), an already-open tab running an old index-*.js that lazy-navigates to a not-yet-visited route requests an old hashed chunk the new precache purged -> 404 -> unhandled chunk-load rejection. The SW navigation fallback only covers navigations, not script assets. Add a global handler that catches dynamic-import/chunk-load failures and triggers a full reload (or a 'new version available, reload' prompt instead of skipWaiting). Surfaced in 156 review; same caveat noted in 135 review.

title: Invalidate existing sessions on password change (tokenVersion in JWT)
description: Password change (142) re-hashes the password but does NOT revoke already-issued tokens: this is stateless JWT with a 24h access + 30d refresh token and no token version/denylist, so an attacker's or lost-device's tokens stay valid up to 30 days after the user changes their password. Add a tokenVersion (or passwordChangedAt) column to User, embed it in access+refresh tokens, verify it in the auth/refresh middleware, and bump it on password change (and ideally on logout-all). This is what makes 'change password' actually able to revoke compromised sessions. Surfaced in ticket 142 review (Important #1).

title: Rate-limit PATCH /users/me/password (current-password check is unthrottled)
description: The auth rate limiter (131) covers /api/auth/* but not /api/users/me/password. An attacker needs a valid victim JWT to reach the bcrypt current-password check, so exposure is low, but if a token leaks the current-password guess is unthrottled. Apply a rate limiter to this endpoint. Minor; from ticket 142 review.

title: Clear React Query cache (queryClient.clear) on logout/account-delete
description: logout() clears tokens + the offline set-log queue but not the React Query cache, so on a shared device stale cached workout/feed data could linger in memory until consumers unmount. Call queryClient.clear() on logout (and account deletion) for a clean teardown. Low severity (memory only, cleared on reload). From ticket 144 review (Minor #4).

title: WorkoutDetail edit: per-set aria-labels + guard resync while focused (a11y/UX polish)
description: From ticket 148 review (minor): all editable reps inputs share aria-label 'Reps performed' (screen readers can't distinguish set 1 vs 3) — label per set (e.g. 'Reps for set N'). Also the value-resync useEffect can briefly clobber the field while focused/typing under an in-flight optimistic update; guard it so it doesn't overwrite during active editing. Both minor.

title: Durable test-isolation: make test-created plans non-public + self-contained stateful social tests
description: Follow-up from the 090/173/174 stabilization review. Two durable fixes so retry isn't load-bearing: (1) Root hazard not fixed at source — admin-plan-editor E2E tests and e2e/helpers/create-second-plan.ts create plans with isPublic defaulting true (admin/plans.ts:59) with no cleanup, polluting the public-plans list any 'select a plan' path sees. The selectFirstPlan->nSuns change routes the fixture around it, but pass isPublic:false from admin/helper-created test plans to kill it at the source. (2) The stateful, order-dependent social tests (social.test.ts Feed reactions toggle-chain ~442-630, friend-request flow ~66-186) share one feedEventId/token across sequential its with exact-count assertions; vitest retry re-runs only the failing it (re-toggling against mutated state), so it can spuriously fail or mask an intermittent regression. Make each assertion self-contained (per-test event/reactions) so retry is unnecessary for them.

title: Plate calculator: clearer message when target weight < bar weight
description: From ticket 004 review (minor). computePlatesPerSide returns empty groups for both weight==bar and weight<bar, so a target lighter than the selected bar shows 'Just the bar — no plates needed', which is misleading (that bar can't make the lighter weight). Distinguish perSide<0 from perSide===0 and show a message like 'Lighter than the selected bar'. Low frequency (main-lift prescribed weights rarely dip below 20kg).
